rationale: Both correctly validate the direct-vs-delegated loader design and both flag the same latent gap (the still-unfixed diffusion-stage hunyuan_image3_transformer.py caller of the removed API), so gap_hit is true for both. Y is better grounded, citing actual upstream vLLM source lines (utils.py, weight_utils.py, fp8.py) to prove the design claim, and its test-coverage critique (delegated-loader test never actually exercises AutoWeightsLoader's mapping) echoes the ground truth's core non-blocking concern about tests only proving the mapper is 'called' rather than truly validated — X misses this entirely. X's parallel finding relies on a suspicious 'gist screenshot in PR body' citation that reads as fabricated flavor, and pads its report with low-value nitpicks (suggesting explanatory comments) that don't map to any real reviewer concern, hurting precision. Both are concretely actionable with file/line anchors, but Y's recommendations are more technically precise and its gap-flag is more measured/accurate (correctly notes it was fixed later) versus X's more speculative 'will raise AttributeError' framing.
